projects.title: PetMatch
projects.description: We have made an interactive pet matching website where users can find or offer pets based on personal preferences. Through a matching algorithm (made with a combination of Javscript and an API), users are matched with suitable pets based on factors such as lifestyle, age, family situation, etc. This makes the adoption process more personal, efficient and user-friendly than traditional adoption sites. The platform goes further than just matching. Users can also get in touch with each other to exchange experiences or ask questions about the animal.

projects.title: Hamster Care
projects.description: Hamster Care is an HTML, CSS and Javscript assignment which I had to create in the first year of the CMD course. Our assignment was to create a fully working tamagotchi game in only HTML, CSS and Javascript. For me this was quite fun! Even though at this point I had little to no experience with Javascript, my prior experience in game development made it easy to understand the code. In no time I made a fully working game with even a shop system!

projects.title: Rubics Cube
projects.description: This Rubics Cube was one of the assignments I had to cretae while following the minor Web Development and Design. While it may not seem like alot on the surface, the actual assignment was way more difficult than I thought. That is because this working rubics cube is made entirely out of HTML5 and CSS3 only. No javascript, no other languages, only CSS and HTML. While this was a very fun project, it was a very very VERY difficult one. One of the very few projects I never got to fully finish.

projects.title: Hackathon Project
projects.description: The Hackton website was an assignment given to us by another company entirely. This company is called SRON, SRON is made entirely of student and is specialised in researched black holes and outer space. They have their own sattelite which they will be launching in 2030, and they asked us and other student to create a website for said sattelite. This was one of my favorite project due to the fact that I have a HIGH passion for outer space. I love everything when it comes to outer space so I had alot of fun researching what we could add to our website.

projects.title: NASA Web API
projects.description: The Nasa Web API assignment is one of the most recent project I had to create. We received the assignment to create a website which makes use of 1 already existing content API, and 2 already existing WEB API's. Although this of itself was not much of a challenge, the bigger challenge was learning a whole new style of coding. We had to make use of ASTRO instead of the usual HTML and CSS. Astro is an online service which gives us to ability to render our website on a server instead of locally. This is very challenging and gives a whole new dimension to coding.

projects.title: FED
projects.description: FED, also called Front End Development speaks for itself, we had to create a website which mostly focused on frontend development. To challenge us, we received the assignment to recreate an already existing website. Due to my prior experience in coding, I decided to challenge myself to a more modern website with alot of animation;  I chose the Koenigsegg website. This website is high class and very modern which made it the perfect challenge. I finished this class with a grade of a solid 9.5

projects.title: Warship
projects.description: Warship was my very last game I created at Media College, it was my final assignment which decided if I would pass or not. Not only was it quite the big assignment, but we also had to create this game for a client. This was'nt just any client but the KM (Koninklijke Marine). There was alot of pressure just because of this client alone, so we had to create something awesome. We came up with the idea to create a turn based battle game where you would have to move the ships strategically across the battlefield. Although this was one of the most difficult tests I had eve taken, we made it the first try and the client was very happy! I was mostly responsible for the UI parts and some battle mechanics withn the game itself.
